description:
You are a build-and-release engineer who has just cloned a tiny demo project located in
    /home/user/artifact-project (the directory exists but is empty right now).

    Your goal is to author a GNU Makefile that automates a complete build / test / package
    workflow while demonstrating the use of *parallel* recipe lines in the “compile” phase.

    Final deliverables that MUST exist **after you run “make all”**:

    1. /home/user/artifact-project/build/mytool  
       • regular text file  
       • exact, single-line contents:  
         int main(){return 0;}

    2. /home/user/artifact-project/reports/test-results.log  
       • regular text file  
       • exact, single-line contents:  
         TEST_SUITE_RESULT: PASS

    3. /home/user/artifact-project/dist/mytool-1.0.0.tar.gz  
       • gzip-compressed tar archive  
       • the *ONLY* two members inside the archive, in this exact order, must be  
         a) build/mytool  
         b) reports/test-results.log

       To be crystal-clear, the command  
       tar -tzf /home/user/artifact-project/dist/mytool-1.0.0.tar.gz  
       must output *exactly* (two newline-terminated lines, no leading “./”):

           build/mytool
           reports/test-results.log

    4. The Makefile itself, stored at /home/user/artifact-project/Makefile, must define at
       least the following phony targets:

       • compile   – produces build/mytool  
         - *Must* launch at least two subshell commands in parallel (backgrounded
           with “&”) and then synchronise them with a single “wait” so that `make -j`
           is *not* required for concurrency.  
         - Each parallel command should print a short “Building …” status line and
           pause for ~1 second using `sleep 1` (simulating two compilation units).

       • test      – generates reports/test-results.log with the exact line specified
                     above.

       • package   – creates dist/, then packages both artefacts into
                     dist/mytool-1.0.0.tar.gz exactly as described.

       • clean     – removes build/, reports/ and dist/.

       • all       – default target that depends on compile, test and package
                     in that order.

    5. The Makefile must also declare `.PHONY: all compile test package clean`.

    Operating constraints & tips:

    • You have standard GNU coreutils and GNU Make on the system.  
    • You do *not* need a C compiler—just create the text file containing the dummy
      “int main(){return 0;}” line to represent the compiled binary.  
    • Do not hard-code absolute paths inside the Makefile; assume it is executed
      from /home/user/artifact-project.  
    • Ensure all intermediate directories (build/, reports/, dist/) are created
      automatically by the corresponding targets.  
    • After you have created the Makefile, invoke the appropriate `make` command(s)
      to leave the workspace in the final state described above so that automated
      grading scripts pass.

    Verification expectations:

    • File and directory names are *case-sensitive*.  
    • No extra files may appear inside the tarball.  
    • Text files must contain the exact lines shown—no additional whitespace.  
    • The timestamps and permissions are irrelevant to grading; only existence
      and exact file contents/ordering are checked.

    When finished, you should be able to run the following commands with the indicated
    results (shown here purely for illustration; the grading container will do the
    same checks automatically):

        $ cd /home/user/artifact-project
        $ make clean && make all
        $ cat build/mytool
        int main(){return 0;}
        $ cat reports/test-results.log
        TEST_SUITE_RESULT: PASS
        $ tar -tzf dist/mytool-1.0.0.tar.gz
        build/mytool
        reports/test-results.log

    <!-- Final expected files after agent runs 'make all' -->
    File: /home/user/artifact-project/build/mytool
    Contents (exact, no trailing newline beyond the one shown):
int main(){return 0;}
    
    File: /home/user/artifact-project/reports/test-results.log
    Contents (exact, no trailing newline beyond the one shown):
TEST_SUITE_RESULT: PASS

    File: /home/user/artifact-project/dist/mytool-1.0.0.tar.gz
    • gzip tar archive
    • tar -tzf output exactly:
build/mytool
reports/test-results.log

    Makefile: /home/user/artifact-project/Makefile
    • Must contain targets “compile”, “test”, “package”, “clean”, “all”
    • compile target must contain two commands backgrounded with “&” and a single “wait”.

    The autograder will:
        1. Check that the Makefile exists.
        2. Run “make -C /home/user/artifact-project clean”.
        3. Run “make -C /home/user/artifact-project all”.
        4. Verify that the three artefacts above exist and match the exact
           contents/structure stipulated.
